hypothesis: If FREED++ optimizes pharmacophoric similarity to a reference set of 53 estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity to promote novelty, then the generated drug-like molecules will exhibit high pharmacophore similarity to the reference set, reduced structural similarity to public databases (ChEMBL, ZINC, PubChem), and docking scores comparable to or better than the baseline, demonstrating an effective balance between pharmacophore alignment, novelty, and target affinity.
